A

HYDRONE Core Development

2019 – 2022 · Four Hardware Generations

2019
GEN 1
Submarine Prototype
First Proof of Concept

Full ballast system RC submarine. Limited to 5–9 inches depth due to 2.4 GHz RF attenuation through water — even at 75 MHz. The first hardware proof-of-concept that established the fundamental challenge of underwater RF communication.

▶ Prototype Collage
📷 Photos2 images▼
Gen 1.1 — 2.4 GHz RC Submarine
Gen 1.2 — 75 MHz RC Submarine
2021
GEN 2
ROV Prototype
Dynamic Thruster ROV

Transitioned to a dynamic thruster ROV. With tether arrangement, persistent RF issues are solved tentatively; control range capped at 2–3 ft underwater. Two sub-variants explored omnidirectional and differential thruster configurations.

▶ Gen 2 Test
📷 Photos4 images▼
Gen 2.1 — Omni-Thruster ROV
Gen 2.2 — Diff-Thruster ROV
Gen 2.1 — Servo Gimbal
Gen 2.2 — Dual BLDC Diff
2021
GEN 3
First Y6 Aerial-Aquatic Fusion
HYDRoNE

Y6 multi-rotor configuration merging drone and ROV. Floating antenna introduced to bypass RF attenuation completely with a new antenna deployment mechanism — the first true aerial-aquatic hybrid vehicle.

▶ Gen 3 Test A ▶ Gen 3 Test B
📷 Photos2 images▼
Gen 3 — HYDRoNE CAD Model
Gen 3 — Physical HYDRoNE Prototype
2022
GEN 4
Quad-Rotor Float Unit
HYDRONE vIVo

Complete redesign as a floating quad-rotor with a fish-finder camera providing 30 m underwater visibility. Antenna extension mechanism eliminated. High payload capacity disc BLDC motors.

▶ Gen 4 Test

B

Propulsion Extensions

Variable Pitch · Differential Gearbox · Dual-Medium

EXT 01
MICKEY
Variable Pitch Dual-Medium Propulsion · 2024

HYDRONE's biggest propulsion bottleneck solved: a fixed pitch propeller for air performs poorly underwater at the same RPM, and vice versa. MICKEY allows blade pitch to change dynamically as the vehicle crosses the air-water interface — maintaining thrust and efficiency in both media with the same motor and RPM.

How It Works

  • Air pitch (53° servo rotation): Flatter-optimized for aerial thrust. Servo-actuated control rod escalates inner hub relative to fixed outer hub.
  • Water pitch (68° servo rotation): Wider-optimized for underwater propulsion using the same mechanism.
  • Moisture sensor detects medium change and signals Arduino, which commands the servo.
  • Validated at 50% throttle — structural integrity and sufficient propulsion in both media confirmed.
▶ MICKEY Demo A ▶ MICKEY Demo B
📷 Photos & CAD8 images▼
Variable Pitch Transition
MICKEY — CAD Model
MICKEY — Physical Setup
Raindrop Sensor → Servo PWM
Full Control Circuit
Actuation Assembly
Thrust Apparatus — Tank Test
Hub — Pitch Mechanism

Next Steps

  • ESC upgrade to 40–60 A for full power testing.
  • Hydrofoil cross section analysis to reduce hub & blade vibration.
  • Weight reduction via optimized infill density in 3D-printed parts.
  • Thrust bearings + friction reduction on connecting rod for cleaner actuation.
EXT 02
TB5
Differential-Gearbox Dual-Medium Propulsion · Ongoing

The upcoming HYDRONE Gen 5 propulsion system uses an automotive-inspired micro bevel gear differential to route power from a single brushless motor to both aerial and marine propellers, replacing traditional pitch-changing mechanisms.

▶ TB5 Demo
📷 CAD2 images▼
TB5 — Bevel Gear Differential
Dual Propeller Mesh
C

Underwater Module

MARINOVA · Industrial ROV System · 2026 Capstone

2026 CAPSTONE PROJECT
2026
CAPSTONE
Industrial ROV System
MARINOVA

Exclusively underwater-focused branch of HYDRONE — a low-cost, tethered ROV built for underwater inspection. Originally developed for UVDC India 2024 as PROJECT AQUA (2nd place globally), then physically realized as a capstone project at a total build cost of 160 USD.

Surface-Relay CommsFloating buoy with VTX & FS-i6 receiver → 3m Cat6 Ethernet tether. Lollipop antenna for smooth FPV video.

Full 6-DoFDJI 920KV BLDC on MG996R servo mounts pivot 0°→90°, achieving complete 6 degrees of freedom thrust vectoring.

WaterproofingM-Seal epoxy + cable glands for wire penetrations; TPU balloons for servos & torch housings.

Passive Safety1 kg cast-iron counterweight → stable low-positive buoyancy. Passively surfaces on power loss.

Environmental SensingpH, salinity, turbidity, dissolved oxygen sensors for underwater environmental monitoring.

Ultra Low CostFull industrial-grade ROV built for just $160 USD.
